name: Classic Margherita Pizza
tagline: Naples-born pizza with crushed tomato, fresh mozzarella, basil
highlight: Crisp-blistered crust under tomato, basil, milky mozz

hero.alt: Margherita pizza with bubbling mozzarella and torn basil leaves
hero.caption: margherita — tomato, mozzarella, basil

healthFacts: No nuts in any component — naturally nut-free, safe for tree-nut allergies.
San Marzano tomatoes are low-acid and high in lycopene; cooking releases more.
Fresh mozzarella has roughly half the sodium of aged cheeses.

steps.title: Preheat the oven
steps.detail: Set oven to 500°F (260°C) with a pizza stone or steel on the middle rack. Heat 30 minutes minimum — the stone needs to be fully saturated for a crisp bottom.
steps.tip: No stone? Invert a heavy sheet pan and preheat that instead.

steps.title: Stretch and dress
steps.detail: On a floured peel, stretch the dough to 12 inches. Spread crushed tomato thinly, leaving a half-inch border. Tear mozzarella into chunks and dot across; finish with olive oil and a pinch of salt.
steps.tip: Tomatoes go on raw — they cook with the pizza. Pre-cooking the sauce makes it bitter.

media.alt: Stretched pizza dough with tomato and mozzarella
media.caption: stretch and dress

steps.title: Bake hot and fast
steps.detail: Slide onto the stone and bake 7–9 minutes until the crust is leopard-spotted and the cheese is bubbling and just starting to brown.
steps.tip: If your oven has a broiler, finish 30 seconds under it for restaurant char.

steps.title: Finish with basil
steps.detail: Pull from the oven, scatter torn basil leaves over the hot pizza, rest 1 minute before slicing — the cheese needs to set briefly so it doesn't slide.
steps.tip: Basil goes on after cooking; it burns to nothing in the oven.
